title: Pluchea cortesii
taxonomy: Pluchea cortesii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pluchea, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as sea lavender or camphor plant. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of plants that also includes daisies, sunflowers, and lettuce. Pluchea cortesii is native to the Philippines and is often found in coastal areas. The classification of Pluchea cortesii is further defined by its classification within the order Asterales.
uses: Pluchea cortesii has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pluchea cortesii for medicinal purposes. Its potential therapeutic benefits are still being studied and explored by scientists.
